executor: Claude Sonnet 5 (Claude Code agent), headless read-only sweep
repoMutations: none — git status before and after this sweep shows only the 2 pre-existing, explicitly out-of-scope strays (mcp-tooling/008-mcp-aside/001-research/research/research.md, system-deep-loop/032-deep-alignment-mode/013-review-remediation/decision-record.md); this archive folder is the only addition

defaultOnHubs.verifiedVia: direct require() of resolve.cjs, DEFAULT_ON_HUBS export, env -u SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING

frozenScorerSha256.note: same 3 cross-hub shared scripts the sk-code playbook-verify-sonnet-20260721-131257 run cited; hashes identical, confirming they are genuinely shared/unmodified across both sweeps

method: Enumerated every file under .opencode/skills/cli-external-orchestration/manual-testing-playbook/ (8 scenario files across hub-routing/, compiled-routing/, and plugins-and-hooks/, plus the root manual-testing-playbook.md directory contract). For the 6 real workflowMode-routing scenarios, ran each scenario's exact documented prompt through: COMPILED — `env -u SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING node .opencode/bin/lib/compiled-routing/011-runtime-engine/lib/resolve.cjs --hub cli-external-orchestration --prompt "<prompt>"`; LEGACY — `node .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/router-replay.cjs --skill .opencode/skills/cli-external-orchestration --task "<same>"`. Compared compiled targets[].workflowMode against legacy intents, cross-checked legacy resources/resourceContract against each scenario's own expected_resources/expected_leaf_resources frontmatter, and confirmed serving authority was genuinely compiled (absence of the resolve.cjs {servingAuthority:'legacy',...} fallback sentinel). For the 2 plugins-and-hooks scenarios (CE-P01, CE-P02) — which the hub's own root playbook explicitly marks 'Unscored... not part of the scored hub-routing corpus' with expected_intent/expected_workflow_mode: UNKNOWN and no parseable prompt (confirmed via load-playbook-scenarios.cjs: prompt:null, parseWarnings:['missing-exact-prompt']) — ran the same compiled/legacy commands with an empty prompt to reproduce the archived Lane C harness's own vacuous defer/defer row for these two IDs, then separately re-executed each scenario's own documented functional test recipe (its real pass criterion) live, fresh, today.

summary.routingDriftObserved: 0/6 meaningful routing scenarios (CE-P01/CE-P02 excluded from this count — vacuous/non-signal-bearing by design, reported separately)

laneCCrossCheck.corroboration: EXACT MATCH. This sweep independently re-derived routing decisions (via manual per-scenario resolve.cjs/router-replay.cjs invocations, not by re-invoking the Lane C harness itself) for all 8 of the same scenario IDs and found 8/8 agreement with the archived report's per-row frontDoorOutcome/status: CE-001 route/cli-opencode, CE-002 route/cli-claude-code, CE-003 defer, CE-H01 route/cli-opencode, CE-H02 route/cli-claude-code, CE-CR-001 route/cli-opencode, CE-P01 defer (vacuous), CE-P02 defer (vacuous). 0 drift confirmed independently.

scenarios.prompt: Delegate this to OpenCode and run the ablation suite with full plugin and Spec Kit Memory runtime.

expected.shape: single dominant mode, not ordered bundle or defer

scenarios.notes: Compiled and legacy agree on cli-opencode as a single dominant mode; matches expected_workflow_mode and both expected_resources entries exactly; matched aliases (opencode, spec kit memory) align with the doc's described 'OpenCode, full plugin runtime, ablation suite' signal.

scenarios.prompt: Get an Anthropic CLI second opinion on this refactor with extended thinking before I merge.

expected.shape: single dominant mode, not ordered bundle or defer

scenarios.notes: Compiled and legacy agree on cli-claude-code as a single dominant mode; matches expected_workflow_mode and both expected_resources entries exactly; matched aliases directly echo the doc's described 'Anthropic CLI, extended thinking' signal.

scenarios.prompt: Dispatch this to a CLI executor.

expected.shape: must ask which executor, not silently default to cli-opencode

scenarios.notes: Both engines defer with 0 matched aliases and defaultApplied:false, confirming genuine ambiguity handling — routerPolicy.defaultMode is null in hub-router.json and neither engine silently picked cli-opencode (tieBreak[0]) on this ambiguous 'CLI executor' phrase.

scenarios.prompt: Hand this whole task to a separate autonomous coding session that has the full plugin and memory stack and let it run unattended.

expected.shape: blind holdout — no literal OpenCode alias in prompt, must still resolve cli-opencode

scenarios.notes: Blind holdout resolves correctly via the opencode-dispatch vocabulary class alone ('full plugin and memory stack' phrase, score 4 vs the 8 alias+dispatch double-match in CE-001) — no literal 'OpenCode' token present, confirming natural-language generalization holds under compiled routing exactly as under legacy.

scenarios.prompt: Get a second, deeply-reasoned opinion on this architecture decision from a different assistant model.

expected.shape: blind holdout — no literal Claude Code alias in prompt, must still resolve cli-claude-code

scenarios.notes: Blind holdout resolves correctly via the claude-dispatch vocabulary class alone ('deeply-reasoned opinion' phrase) — no literal 'Claude Code' token present, confirming natural-language generalization holds under compiled routing exactly as under legacy.

scenarios.prompt: Delegate this to OpenCode and run the ablation suite with full plugin and Spec Kit Memory runtime.
scenarios.notes: Prompt is textually identical to CE-001's. PASS per this scenario's own explicit Pass/Fail Criteria section, which tests only servingAuthority:compiled (confirmed — resolve.cjs returned a real route object, not the {servingAuthority:'legacy',...} fallback sentinel) and compiled-workflow-mode==legacy-workflow-mode (both cli-opencode). FINDING (non-blocking): the frontmatter field route_shape:orderedBundle does not match the live-observed selectionKind:single for this identical single-executor prompt — orderedBundle is a distinct, real selectionKind in this codebase (fires when 2+ workflow modes are explicitly requested in one prompt, e.g. sibling hubs' mcp-tooling/system-deep-loop compiled-routing scenarios that genuinely request two modes) but this scenario's prompt only ever requests one executor (OpenCode). Reads as a copy-paste/labeling artifact from the shared cross-hub compiled-routing scenario template rather than a routing defect — the scenario's own stated Pass/Fail Criteria never references route_shape or selectionKind, only servingAuthority + workflow-mode agreement, both of which hold. Flagged for documentation hygiene, not actioned (read-only scope).
